v.CNV: 有一天，耶稣在殿里教导人，宣讲福音，众祭司长、经学家和长老上前来，
v.ESV: One day, as Jesus was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes with the elders came up
v.GAE: 하루는 예수께서 성전에서 백성을 가르치시며 복음을 전하실새 대제사장들과 서기관들이 장로들과 함께 가까이 와서
v.KJV: And it came to pass, that on one of those days, as he taught the people in the temple, and preached the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes came upon him with the elders,
v.NASB: On one of the days while He was teaching the people in the temple and preaching the gospel, the chief priests and the scribes with the elders confronted Him,
v.NIV: One day as Jesus was teaching the people in the temple courts and proclaiming the good news, the chief priests and the teachers of the law, together with the elders, came up to him.
v.NRSV: One day, as he was teaching the people in the temple and telling the good news, the chief priests and the scribes came with the elders
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 어느 날 성전에서 백성을 가르치시며, 기쁜 소식을 전하고 계실 때에, 대제사장들과 율법학자들이 장로들과 함께 예수께 와서
v.WLB: 예수께서 성전에서 사람들을 가르치며 복음을 전하고 계실 때에 대제사장들과 율법학자들이 장로들과 함께 예수께 다가와

v.CNV: 对他说：“告诉我们，你凭着什么权柄作这些事？给你这权柄的是谁？”
v.ESV: and said to him, “Tell us by what authority you do these things, or who it is that gave you this authority.”
v.GAE: 말하여 이르되 당신이 무슨 권위로 이런 일을 하는지 이 권위를 준 이가 누구인지 우리에게 말하라
v.KJV: And spake unto him, saying, Tell us, by what authority doest thou these things? or who is he that gave thee this authority?
v.NASB: and they spoke, saying to Him, 'Tell us by what authority You are doing these things, or who is the one who gave You this authority?'
v.NIV: “Tell us by what authority you are doing these things,” they said. “Who gave you this authority?”
v.NRSV: and said to him, “Tell us, by what authority are you doing these things? Who is it who gave you this authority?”
v.SAENEW: 말하였다. "당신은 무슨 권한으로 이런 일을 합니까? 누가 이런 권한을 당신에게 주었습니까? 어디 우리에게 말해 보십시오."
v.WLB: 말했습니다. “당신이 무슨 권세로 이런 일을 하는 것인지 말해 보시오. 누가 이런 권세를 주었소?”

v.CNV: 他回答他们：“我也要问你们一句话，你们告诉我：
v.ESV: He answered them, “I also will ask you a question. Now tell me,
v.GAE: 대답하여 이르시되 나도 한 말을 너희에게 물으리니 내게 말하라
v.KJV: And he answered and said unto them, I will also ask you one thing; and answer me:
v.NASB: Jesus answered and said to them, 'I will also ask you a question, and you tell Me:
v.NIV: He replied, “I will also ask you a question. Tell me:
v.NRSV: He answered them, “I will also ask you a question, and you tell me:
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 대답하셨다. "나도 너희에게 한 가지 물어 보겠으니, 나에게 대답해 보아라.
v.WLB: 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “나도 한 가지 물어보겠으니 내게 말하라.

v.CNV: 约翰的洗礼是从天上来的，还是从人来的呢？”
v.ESV: was the baptism of John from heaven or from man?”
v.GAE: 요한의 세례가 하늘로부터냐 사람으로부터냐
v.KJV: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or of men?
v.NASB: 'Was the baptism of John from heaven or from men?'
v.NIV: John’s baptism —was it from heaven, or of human origin?”
v.NRSV: Did the baptism of John come from heaven, or was it of human origin?”
v.SAENEW: 요한의 세례가 하늘에서 난 것이냐? 사람에게서 난 것이냐?"
v.WLB: 요한의 세례가 하늘로부터 왔느냐? 아니면 사람으로부터 왔느냐?”

v.CNV: 他们就彼此议论说：“如果我们说：‘是从天上来的’，他就会问：‘那你们为什么不相信他呢？’
v.ESV: And they discussed it with one another, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why did you not believe him?’
v.GAE: 그들이 서로 의논하여 이르되 만일 하늘로부터라 하면 어찌하여 그를 믿지 아니하였느냐 할 것이요
v.KJV: And they reasoned with themselves, saying, If we shall say, From heaven; he will say, Why then believed ye him not?
v.NASB: They reasoned among themselves, saying, 'If we say, 'From heaven,' He will say, 'Why did you not believe him?'
v.NIV: They discussed it among themselves and said, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will ask, ‘Why didn’t you believe him?’
v.NRSV: They discussed it with one another, saying, “If we say, ‘From heaven,’ he will say, ‘Why did you not believe him?’
v.SAENEW: 그들은 자기들끼리 의논하면서 말하였다. "'하늘에서 났다'고 말하면, '어찌하여 그를 믿지 않았느냐'고 할 것이요,
v.WLB: 그들은 서로 의논하며 말했습니다. “만약 우리가 ‘하늘로부터 왔다’고 하면 예수가 ‘그러면 왜 그를 믿지 않았느냐’고 할 것이고

v.CNV: 如果我们说：‘是从人来的’，众人都会用石头打我们，因为他们都认定约翰是先知。”
v.ESV: But if we say, ‘From man,’ all the people will stone us to death, for they are convinced that John was a prophet.”
v.GAE: 만일 사람으로부터라 하면 백성이 요한을 선지자로 인정하니 그들이 다 우리를 돌로 칠 것이라 하고
v.KJV: But and if we say, Of men; all the people will stone us: for they be persuaded that John was a prophet.
v.NASB: 'But if we say, 'From men,' all the people will stone us to death, for they are convinced that John was a prophet.'
v.NIV: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ all the people will stone us, because they are persuaded that John was a prophet.”
v.NRSV: But if we say, ‘Of human origin,’ all the people will stone us; for they are convinced that John was a prophet.”
v.SAENEW: '사람에게서 났다'고 말하면, 온 백성이 요한을 예언자로 믿고 있으니, 그들이 우리를 돌로 칠 것이다."
v.WLB: ‘사람으로부터 왔다’고 하면 요한을 예언자라고 믿는 모든 백성들이 우리에게 돌을 던질 것이다.”

v.CNV: 于是他们回答耶稣：“我们不知道是从哪里来的。”
v.ESV: So they answered that they did not know where it came from.
v.GAE: 대답하되 어디로부터인지 알지 못하노라 하니
v.KJV: And they answered, that they could not tell whence it was.
v.NASB: So they answered that they did not know where it came from.
v.NIV: So they answered, “We don’t know where it was from.”
v.NRSV: So they answered that they did not know where it came from.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 그들은 요한의 세례가 어디에서 났는지를 모른다고 대답하였다.
v.WLB: 그래서 그들은 대답했습니다. “어디로부터 왔는지 우리는 모르겠다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣说：“我也不告诉你们，我凭着什么权柄作这些事。”
v.ESV: And Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I do these things.”
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 나도 무슨 권위로 이런 일을 하는지 너희에게 이르지 아니하리라 하시니라
v.KJV: And Jesus said unto them, Neither tell I you by what authority I do these things.
v.NASB: And Jesus said to them, 'Nor will I tell you by what authority I do these things.'
v.NIV: Jesus said, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”
v.NRSV: Then Jesus said to them, “Neither will I tell you by what authority I am doing these things.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "나도 무슨 권한으로 이런 일을 하는지를 너희에게 말하지 않겠다."
v.WLB: 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “그렇다면 나도 무슨 권세로 이런 일을 하는지 너희에게 말하지 않겠다.”

v.CNV: 于是耶稣向众人讲了这个比喻：“有一个人栽种了一个葡萄园，租给佃户，就远行去了很久。
v.ESV: And he began to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard and let it out to tenants and went into another country for a long while.
v.GAE: 그가 또 이 비유로 백성에게 말씀하시기 시작하시니라 한 사람이 포도원을 만들어 농부들에게 세로 주고 타국에 가서 오래 있다가
v.KJV: Then began he to speak to the people this parable; A certain man planted a vineyard, and let it forth to husbandmen, and went into a far country for a long time.
v.NASB: And He began to tell the people this parable: 'A man planted a vineyard and rented it out to vine-growers, and went on a journey for a long time.
v.NIV: He went on to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard, rented it to some farmers and went away for a long time.
v.NRSV: He began to tell the people this parable: “A man planted a vineyard, and leased it to tenants, and went to another country for a long time.
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 백성에게 이 비유를 말씀하셨다. "어떤 사람이 포도원을 만들어서, 농부들에게 세로 주고, 오랫동안 멀리 떠나 있었다.
v.WLB: 예수께서는 사람들에게 이런 비유를 들려주셨습니다. “어떤 사람이 포도원을 만들어 농부들에게 세를 주고 오랫동안 떠나 있었다.

v.CNV: 到了时候，园主派了一个仆人到佃户那里，叫他们把葡萄园当纳的果子交给他。佃户却打了他，放他空手回去。
v.ESV: When the time came, he sent a servant to the tenants, so that they would give him some of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
v.GAE: 때가 이르매 포도원 소출 얼마를 바치게 하려고 한 종을 농부들에게 보내니 농부들이 종을 몹시 때리고 거저 보내었거늘
v.KJV: And at the season he sent a servant to the husbandmen, that they should give him of the fruit of the vineyard: but the husbandmen beat him, and sent him away empty.
v.NASB: 'At the harvest time he sent a slave to the vine-growers, so that they would give him some of the produce of the vineyard; but the vine-growers beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
v.NIV: At harvest time he sent a servant to the tenants so they would give him some of the fruit of the vineyard. But the tenants beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
v.NRSV: When the season came, he sent a slave to the tenants in order that they might give him his share of the produce of the vineyard; but the tenants beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
v.SAENEW: 포도를 거둘 때가 되어서, 포도원 주인은 포도원 소출 가운데서 얼마를 소작료로 받아 오게 하려고, 종 하나를 농부들에게 보냈다. 그런데 농부들은 그 종을 때리고, 빈손으로 돌려보냈다.
v.WLB: 포도를 수확할 때가 되자 그는 종을 농부들에게 보내 열매 소출의 얼마를 받아 오라고 했다. 그러나 농부들은 그를 때리고 빈손으로 보내 버렸다.

v.CNV: 园主又派另一个仆人去，佃户又打了他，并且侮辱他，也叫他空手回去。
v.ESV: And he sent another servant. But they also beat and treated him shamefully, and sent him away empty-handed.
v.GAE: 다시 다른 종을 보내니 그도 몹시 때리고 능욕하고 거저 보내었거늘
v.KJV: And again he sent another servant: and they beat him also, and entreated him shamefully, and sent him away empty.
v.NASB: 'And he proceeded to send another slave; and they beat him also and treated him shamefully and sent him away empty-handed.
v.NIV: He sent another servant, but that one also they beat and treated shamefully and sent away empty-handed.
v.NRSV: Next he sent another slave; that one also they beat and insulted and sent away empty-handed.
v.SAENEW: 주인은 다른 종을 보냈다. 그랬더니 그들은 그 종도 때리고, 모욕하고, 빈손으로 돌려보냈다.
v.WLB: 주인은 다른 종을 보냈다. 그러나 그들은 그 종도 때리고 모욕하고 빈손으로 돌려보냈다.

v.CNV: 于是他又派第三个去，佃户把他打伤，赶他走了。
v.ESV: And he sent yet a third. This one also they wounded and cast out.
v.GAE: 다시 세 번째 종을 보내니 이 종도 상하게 하고 내쫓은지라
v.KJV: And again he sent a third: and they wounded him also, and cast him out.
v.NASB: 'And he proceeded to send a third; and this one also they wounded and cast out.
v.NIV: He sent still a third, and they wounded him and threw him out.
v.NRSV: And he sent still a third; this one also they wounded and threw out.
v.SAENEW: 그래서 주인이 다시 세 번째 종을 보냈더니, 그들은 이 종에게도 상처를 입혀서 내쫓았다.
v.WLB: 주인은 세 번째 종을 보냈지만 농부들은 그 종마저 상처를 입혀 쫓아 보냈다.

v.CNV: 葡萄园的主人说：‘怎么办呢？我要派我的爱子去，也许这一个他们会尊敬的。’
v.ESV: Then the owner of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will respect him.’
v.GAE: 포도원 주인이 이르되 어찌할까 내 사랑하는 아들을 보내리니 그들이 혹 그는 존대하리라 하였더니
v.KJV: Then said the lord of the vineyard, What shall I do? I will send my beloved son: it may be they will reverence him when they see him.
v.NASB: 'The owner of the vineyard said, 'What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will respect him.'
v.NIV: “Then the owner of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my son, whom I love; perhaps they will respect him.’
v.NRSV: Then the owner of the vineyard said, ‘What shall I do? I will send my beloved son; perhaps they will respect him.’
v.SAENEW: 그래서 포도원 주인은 말하였다. '어떻게 할까? 내 사랑하는 아들을 보내야겠다. 설마 그들이 내 아들이야 존중하겠지!'
v.WLB: 그러자 포도원 주인이 말했다. ‘이제 어떻게 할까? 사랑하는 내 아들을 보내야겠다. 아마도 내 아들은 존중하겠지.’

v.CNV: 佃户一看他，就彼此商议说：‘这是继承产业的，让我们杀了他，那么，产业就成为我们的了。’
v.ESV: But when the tenants saw him, they said to themselves, ‘This is the heir. Let us kill him, so that the inheritance may be ours.’
v.GAE: 농부들이 그를 보고 서로 의논하여 이르되 이는 상속자니 죽이고 그 유산을 우리의 것으로 만들자 하고
v.KJV: But when the husbandmen saw him, they reasoned among themselves, saying, This is the heir: come, let us kill him, that the inheritance may be ours.
v.NASB: 'But when the vine-growers saw him, they reasoned with one another, saying, 'This is the heir; let us kill him so that the inheritance will be ours.'
v.NIV: “But when the tenants saw him, they talked the matter over. ‘This is the heir,’ they said. ‘Let’s kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
v.NRSV: But when the tenants saw him, they discussed it among themselves and said, ‘This is the heir; let us kill him so that the inheritance may be ours.’
v.SAENEW: 그러나 농부들은 그를 보고서, 서로 의논하며 말하였다. '이 사람은 상속자다. 그를 죽여 버리자. 그래서 유산이 우리 차지가 되게 하자.'
v.WLB: 그러나 농부들은 그를 보자 서로 의논하며 말했다. ‘이 사람은 상속자니 그를 죽이자. 그러면 그 유산이 우리 것이 될 것이다.’

v.CNV: 于是他们把园主的爱子推出葡萄园外杀了。主人要怎样对待他们呢？
v.ESV: And they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
v.GAE: 포도원 밖에 내쫓아 죽였느니라 그런즉 포도원 주인이 이 사람들을 어떻게 하겠느냐
v.KJV: So they cast him out of the vineyard, and killed him. What therefore shall the lord of the vineyard do unto them?
v.NASB: 'So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What, then, will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
v.NIV: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. “What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
v.NRSV: So they threw him out of the vineyard and killed him. What then will the owner of the vineyard do to them?
v.SAENEW: 그리하여 그들은 주인의 아들을 포도원 바깥으로 내쫓아서 죽였다. 그러니 포도원 주인이 그들을 어떻게 하겠느냐?
v.WLB: 이렇게 해서 그들은 아들을 포도원 밖으로 끌어내 죽였다. 그렇다면 이 포도원 주인은 그들에게 어떻게 하겠느냐?

v.CNV: 他要来除掉这些佃户，把葡萄园租给别人。”他们听见了就说：“千万不要有这样的事发生！”
v.ESV: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.” When they heard this, they said, “Surely not!”
v.GAE: 와서 그 농부들을 진멸하고 포도원을 다른 사람들에게 주리라 하시니 사람들이 듣고 이르되 그렇게 되지 말아지이다 하거늘
v.KJV: He shall come and destroy these husbandmen, and shall give the vineyard to others. And when they heard it, they said, God forbid.
v.NASB: 'He will come and destroy these vine-growers and will give the vineyard to others.' When they heard it, they said, 'May it never be!'
v.NIV: He will come and kill those tenants and give the vineyard to others.” When the people heard this, they said, “God forbid!”
v.NRSV: He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.” When they heard this, they said, “Heaven forbid!”
v.SAENEW: 주인은 와서 그들을 죽이고, 포도원을 다른 사람들에게 줄 것이다." 사람들이 이 말씀을 듣고서 말하였다. "그런 일이 없기를 바랍니다."
v.WLB: 주인이 와서 그 농부들을 죽이고 포도원을 다른 농부들에게 줄 것이다.” 사람들은 이 이야기를 듣고 말했습니다. “이런 일은 절대 없었으면 좋겠습니다.”

v.CNV: 耶稣看着他们，说：“那么，‘建筑工人所弃的石头，成了房角的主要石头’，这经文是什么意思呢？
v.ESV: But he looked directly at them and said, “What then is this that is written: “‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone’?
v.GAE: 그들을 보시며 이르시되 그러면 기록된 바 건축자들의 버린 돌이 모퉁이의 머릿돌이 되었느니라 함이 어찜이냐
v.KJV: And he beheld them, and said, What is this then that is written, The stone which the builders rejected, the same is become the head of the corner?
v.NASB: But Jesus looked at them and said, 'What then is this that is written: 'THE STONE WHICH THE BUILDERS REJECTED, THIS BECAME THE CHIEF CORNER stone'?
v.NIV: Jesus looked directly at them and asked, “Then what is the meaning of that which is written: “‘The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone’ ?
v.NRSV: But he looked at them and said, “What then does this text mean: ‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone’?
v.SAENEW: 그 때에 예수께서 그들을 똑바로 바라보시고 말씀하셨다. "그러면, '집 짓는 사람들이 버린 돌이 집 모퉁이의 머릿돌이 되었다' 하고 기록된 말은 무슨 뜻이냐?
v.WLB: 예수께서는 그들을 똑바로 쳐다보고 말씀하셨습니다. “그렇다면 ‘건축자들이 버린 돌이 모퉁이의 머릿돌이 됐다’라고 기록된 말씀이 무슨 뜻이겠느냐?

v.CNV: 凡是跌在那石头上的，就必摔碎；那石头掉在谁的身上，就必把他压得粉碎。”
v.ESV: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces, and when it falls on anyone, it will crush him.”
v.GAE: 무릇 이 돌 위에 떨어지는 자는 깨어지겠고 이 돌이 사람 위에 떨어지면 그를 가루로 만들어 흩으리라 하시니라
v.KJV: Whosoever shall fall upon that stone shall be broken; but on whomsoever it shall fall, it will grind him to powder.
v.NASB: 'Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces; but on whomever it falls, it will scatter him like dust.'
v.NIV: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces; anyone on whom it falls will be crushed.”
v.NRSV: Everyone who falls on that stone will be broken to pieces; and it will crush anyone on whom it falls.”
v.SAENEW: 누구든지 그 돌 위에 떨어지면, 그는 부스러질 것이요, 그 돌이 어느 사람 위에 떨어지면 그를 가루로 만들 것이다."
v.WLB: 누구든지 이 돌 위에 떨어지는 사람마다 부서질 것이며 이 돌이 어느 사람 위에 떨어지든지 맞는 사람은 가루가 되고 말 것이다.”

v.CNV: 经学家和祭司长知道这比喻是针对他们说的，当时就想动手拿他，可是又害怕群众。
v.ESV: The scribes and the chief priests sought to lay hands on him at that very hour, for they perceived that he had told this parable against them, but they feared the people.
v.GAE: 서기관들과 대제사장들이 예수의 이 비유는 자기들을 가리켜 말씀하심인 줄 알고 즉시 잡고자 하되 백성을 두려워하더라
v.KJV: And the chief priests and the scribes the same hour sought to lay hands on him; and they feared the people: for they perceived that he had spoken this parable against them.
v.NASB: The scribes and the chief priests tried to lay hands on Him that very hour, and they feared the people; for they understood that He spoke this parable against them.
v.NIV: The teachers of the law and the chief priests looked for a way to arrest him immediately, because they knew he had spoken this parable against them. But they were afraid of the people.
v.NRSV: When the scribes and chief priests realized that he had told this parable against them, they wanted to lay hands on him at that very hour, but they feared the people.
v.SAENEW: 율법학자들과 대제사장들은 예수가 자기네들을 겨냥하여 이 비유를 말씀하신 줄 알았다. 그래서 그들은 바로 그 때에 예수께 손을 대어 잡으려고 하였으나, 백성을 두려워하였다.
v.WLB: 율법학자들과 대제사장들은 이 비유가 자기들을 가리켜 하시는 말씀인 것을 알고 당장 예수를 체포할 방도를 모색했습니다. 그러나 그들은 백성들을 두려워했습니다.

v.CNV: 经学家和祭司长差派奸细伪装好人去窥探耶稣，要抓着他的把柄，好把他交由总督全权裁决。
v.ESV: So they watched him and sent spies, who pretended to be sincere, that they might catch him in something he said, so as to deliver him up to the authority and jurisdiction of the governor.
v.GAE: 이에 그들이 엿보다가 예수를 총독의 다스림과 권세 아래에 넘기려 하여 정탐들을 보내어 그들로 스스로 의인인 체하며 예수의 말을 책잡게 하니
v.KJV: And they watched him, and sent forth spies, which should feign themselves just men, that they might take hold of his words, that so they might deliver him unto the power and authority of the governor.
v.NASB: So they watched Him, and sent spies who pretended to be righteous, in order that they might catch Him in some statement, so that they could deliver Him to the rule and the authority of the governor.
v.NIV: Keeping a close watch on him, they sent spies, who pretended to be sincere. They hoped to catch Jesus in something he said, so that they might hand him over to the power and authority of the governor.
v.NRSV: So they watched him and sent spies who pretended to be honest, in order to trap him by what he said, so as to hand him over to the jurisdiction and authority of the governor.
v.SAENEW: 그리하여 그들은 기회를 엿보다가, 정탐꾼들을 보내서, 이들이 거짓으로 의로운 사람들인 체 행세하면서 예수께로 접근하게 하여, 그의 말씀을 책잡게 하였다. 그렇게 해서, 그들은 예수를 총독의 치리권과 사법권에 넘겨주려고 하였다.
v.WLB: 기회를 엿보던 그들은 첩자들을 보내어 의로운 사람들인 체 행동하도록 꾸미게 했습니다. 그리고 예수의 말씀에 트집을 잡아 결국 권력 있는 총독에게 넘겨주려는 속셈이었습니다.

v.CNV: 奸细问他：“老师，我们知道你所说所教的都对，并且不讲情面，只照着真理把　神的道教导人。
v.ESV: So they asked him, “Teacher, we know that you speak and teach rightly, and show no partiality, but truly teach the way of God.
v.GAE: 그들이 물어 이르되 선생님이여 우리가 아노니 당신은 바로 말씀하시고 가르치시며 사람을 외모로 취하지 아니하시고 오직 진리로써 하나님의 도를 가르치시나이다
v.KJV: And they asked him, saying, Master, we know that thou sayest and teachest rightly, neither acceptest thou the person of any, but teachest the way of God truly:
v.NASB: They questioned Him, saying, 'Teacher, we know that You speak and teach correctly, and You are not partial to any, but teach the way of God in truth.
v.NIV: So the spies questioned him: “Teacher, we know that you speak and teach what is right, and that you do not show partiality but teach the way of God in accordance with the truth.
v.NRSV: So they asked him, “Teacher, we know that you are right in what you say and teach, and you show deference to no one, but teach the way of God in accordance with truth.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 예수께 이렇게 물었다. "선생님, 우리는 선생님이, 바르게 말씀하시고, 가르치시고, 또 사람을 겉모양으로 가리지 않으시고, 하나님의 길을 참되게 가르치고 계시는 줄 압니다.
v.WLB: 첩자들이 예수께 물었습니다. “선생님, 선생님의 말씀과 가르침이 옳은 줄 저희가 압니다. 또 사람을 겉모습으로 판단하지 않고 언제나 진리에 따라 하나님의 길을 가르치시는 것도 압니다.

v.CNV: 我们纳税给凯撒可以不可以？”
v.ESV: Is it lawful for us to give tribute to Caesar, or not?”
v.GAE: 우리가 가이사에게 세를 바치는 것이 옳으니이까 옳지 않으니이까 하니
v.KJV: Is it lawful for us to give tribute unto Caesar, or no?
v.NASB: 'Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?'
v.NIV: Is it right for us to pay taxes to Caesar or not?”
v.NRSV: Is it lawful for us to pay taxes to the emperor, or not?”
v.SAENEW: 우리가 황제에게 세금을 바치는 것이 옳습니까, 옳지 않습니까?"
v.WLB: 저희가 가이사에게 세금을 내는 것이 옳습니까, 옳지 않습니까?”

v.CNV: 耶稣看透他们的诡计，就对他们说：
v.ESV: But he perceived their craftiness, and said to them,
v.GAE: 예수께서 그 간계를 아시고 이르시되
v.KJV: But he perceived their craftiness, and said unto them, Why tempt ye me?
v.NASB: But He detected their trickery and said to them,
v.NIV: He saw through their duplicity and said to them,
v.NRSV: But he perceived their craftiness and said to them,
v.SAENEW: 예수께서는 그들의 속셈을 알아채시고서 그들에게 말씀하셨다.
v.WLB: 예수께서 그들의 겉과 속이 다름을 꿰뚫어보고 말씀하셨습니다.

v.CNV: “给我一个银币看看，上面是谁的像，谁的名号？”他们说：“凯撒的。”
v.ESV: “Show me a denarius. Whose likeness and inscription does it have?” They said, “Caesar's.”
v.GAE: 데나리온 하나를 내게 보이라 누구의 형상과 글이 여기 있느냐 대답하되 가이사의 것이니이다
v.KJV: Shew me a penny. Whose image and superscription hath it? They answered and said, Caesar’s.
v.NASB: 'Show Me a denarius. Whose likeness and inscription does it have?' They said, 'Caesar's.'
v.NIV: “Show me a denarius. Whose image and inscription are on it?” “Caesar’s,” they replied.
v.NRSV: “Show me a denarius. Whose head and whose title does it bear?” They said, “The emperor’s.”
v.SAENEW: "데나리온 한 닢을 나에게 보여다오. 이 돈에 누구의 얼굴상과 글자가 새겨져 있느냐?" 그들이 대답하였다. "황제의 것입니다."
v.WLB: “데나리온을 나에게 보여 다오. 동전에 있는 얼굴과 새겨진 글자가 누구의 것이냐?”

v.CNV: 耶稣说：“这样，凯撒的应当归给凯撒，　神的应当归给　神。”
v.ESV: He said to them, “Then render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.”
v.GAE: 이르시되 그런즉 가이사의 것은 가이사에게, 하나님의 것은 하나님께 바치라 하시니
v.KJV: And he said unto them, Render therefore unto Caesar the things which be Caesar’s, and unto God the things which be God’s.
v.NASB: And He said to them, 'Then render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.'
v.NIV: He said to them, “Then give back to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s.”
v.NRSV: He said to them, “Then give to the emperor the things that are the emperor’s, and to God the things that are God’s.”
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "그러면 황제의 것은 황제에게 돌려주고, 하나님의 것은 하나님께 돌려드려라."
v.WLB: 그들은 “가이사의 것입니다”라고 대답했습니다. 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “그렇다면 가이사의 것은 가이사에게 바치고 하나님의 것은 하나님께 바치라.”

v.CNV: 他们在群众面前，不能抓住耶稣的把柄，又希奇他的回答，就沉默下来。
v.ESV: And they were not able in the presence of the people to catch him in what he said, but marveling at his answer they became silent.
v.GAE: 그들이 백성 앞에서 그의 말을 능히 책잡지 못하고 그의 대답을 놀랍게 여겨 침묵하니라
v.KJV: And they could not take hold of his words before the people: and they marvelled at his answer, and held their peace.
v.NASB: And they were unable to catch Him in a saying in the presence of the people; and being amazed at His answer, they became silent.
v.NIV: They were unable to trap him in what he had said there in public. And astonished by his answer, they became silent.
v.NRSV: And they were not able in the presence of the people to trap him by what he said; and being amazed by his answer, they became silent.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 백성 앞에서 예수의 말씀을 책잡지 못하고, 그의 답변에 놀라서 입을 다물었다.
v.WLB: 그들은 사람들 앞에서 예수의 말씀에 트집을 잡을 수 없었습니다. 오히려 예수의 대답에 놀라 말문이 막혀 버렸습니다.

v.CNV: 撒都该人向来否认有复活的事，他们当中有几个人来问耶稣：
v.ESV: There came to him some Sadducees, those who deny that there is a resurrection,
v.GAE: 부활이 없다고 주장하는 사두개인 중 어떤 이들이 와서
v.KJV: Then came to him certain of the Sadducees, which deny that there is any resurrection; and they asked him,
v.NASB: Now there came to Him some of the Sadducees (who say that there is no resurrection),
v.NIV: Some of the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to Jesus with a question.
v.NRSV: Some Sadducees, those who say there is no resurrection, came to him
v.SAENEW: 부활이 없다고 주장하는 사두개파 사람 가운데 몇 사람이 다가와서, 예수께 물었다.
v.WLB: 부활이란 없다고 말하는 어떤 사두개파 사람들이 찾아와 예수께 질문했습니다.

v.CNV: “老师，摩西曾写给我们说：‘如果一个人娶了妻子，还没有儿女就死了，他弟弟就应当娶他的妻子，为哥哥立后。’
v.ESV: and they asked him a question, saying, “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies, having a wife but no children, the man must take the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.
v.GAE: 물어 이르되 선생님이여 모세가 우리에게 써 주기를 만일 어떤 사람의 형이 아내를 두고 자식이 없이 죽으면 그 동생이 그 아내를 취하여 형을 위하여 상속자를 세울지니라 하였나이다
v.KJV: Saying, Master, Moses wrote unto us, If any man’s brother die, having a wife, and he die without children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up seed unto his brother.
v.NASB: and they questioned Him, saying, 'Teacher, Moses wrote for us that IF A MAN'S BROTHER DIES, having a wife, AND HE IS CHILDLESS, HIS BROTHER SHOULD MARRY THE WIFE AND RAISE UP CHILDREN TO HIS BROTHER.
v.NIV: “Teacher,” they said, “Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man must marry the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.
v.NRSV: and asked him a question, “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies, leaving a wife but no children, the man shall marry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
v.SAENEW: "선생님, 모세가 우리에게 써 주기를 '어떤 사람의 형이 자식이 없이 아내를 남겨 두고 죽으면, 그 동생이 그 형수를 맞아들여서 뒤를 이을 아들을 자기 형에게 세워주어야 한다' 하였습니다.
v.WLB: 그들이 말했습니다. “선생님, 모세의 기록에 따르면 ‘형이 자식 없이 아내를 두고 죽으면 그 동생이 과부가 된 형수와 결혼해 형 대신 자식을 낳아야 한다’라고 했습니다.

v.CNV: 从前有兄弟七人，头一个娶了妻子，没有孩子就死了。
v.ESV: Now there were seven brothers. The first took a wife, and died without children.
v.GAE: 그런데 칠 형제가 있었는데 맏이가 아내를 취하였다가 자식이 없이 죽고
v.KJV: There were therefore seven brethren: and the first took a wife, and died without children.
v.NASB: 'Now there were seven brothers; and the first took a wife and died childless;
v.NIV: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married a woman and died childless.
v.NRSV: Now there were seven brothers; the first married, and died childless;
v.SAENEW: 그런데 일곱 형제가 있었습니다. 맏이가 아내를 얻어서 살다가 자식이 없이 죽었습니다.
v.WLB: 그런데 일곱 형제가 있었습니다. 첫째가 아내를 얻어서 살다가 자식 없이 죽었습니다.

v.CNV: 第二个、
v.ESV: And the second
v.GAE: 그 둘째와 셋째가 그를 취하고
v.KJV: And the second took her to wife, and he died childless.
v.NASB: and the second
v.NIV: The second
v.NRSV: then the second
v.SAENEW: 그래서 둘째가 그 여자를 맞아들였고,
v.WLB: 그리고 둘째가 형수를 아내로 얻어 살다가 역시 자식 없이 죽었습니다.

v.CNV: 第三个，也娶过她；七个都是这样，都没有留下孩子就死了。
v.ESV: and the third took her, and likewise all seven left no children and died.
v.GAE: 일곱이 다 그와 같이 자식이 없이 죽고
v.KJV: And the third took her; and in like manner the seven also: and they left no children, and died.
v.NASB: and the third married her; and in the same way all seven died, leaving no children.
v.NIV: and then the third married her, and in the same way the seven died, leaving no children.
v.NRSV: and the third married her, and so in the same way all seven died childless.
v.SAENEW: 그 다음에 셋째가 그 여자를 맞아들였습니다. 일곱 형제가 다 그렇게 하였는데, 모두 자식을 남기지 못하고 죽었습니다.
v.WLB: 그 다음에 셋째가 그 형수와 결혼했고 이런 식으로 해서 일곱째까지 다 자식 없이 죽었습니다.

v.CNV: 后来那女人也死了。
v.ESV: Afterward the woman also died.
v.GAE: 그 후에 여자도 죽었나이다
v.KJV: Last of all the woman died also.
v.NASB: 'Finally the woman died also.
v.NIV: Finally, the woman died too.
v.NRSV: Finally the woman also died.
v.SAENEW: 나중에 그 여자도 죽었습니다.
v.WLB: 그리고 마침내 그 여인도 죽었습니다.

v.CNV: 那么，这女人在复活的时候，要作哪一个的妻子呢？因为七个人都娶过她。”
v.ESV: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife will the woman be? For the seven had her as wife.”
v.GAE: 일곱이 다 그를 아내로 취하였으니 부활 때에 그 중에 누구의 아내가 되리이까
v.KJV: Therefore in the resurrection whose wife of them is she? for seven had her to wife.
v.NASB: 'In the resurrection therefore, which one's wife will she be? For all seven had married her.'
v.NIV: Now then, at the resurrection whose wife will she be, since the seven were married to her?”
v.NRSV: In the resurrection, therefore, whose wife will the woman be? For the seven had married her.”
v.SAENEW: 그러니 부활 때에 그 여자는 그들 가운데서 누구의 아내가 되겠습니까? 일곱이 다 그 여자를 아내로 맞아들였으니 말입니다."
v.WLB: 그렇다면 일곱 형제가 모두 이 여인과 결혼했으니 부활 때 이 여인은 누구의 아내가 되는 것입니까?”

v.CNV: 耶稣说：“这世界的人又娶又嫁，
v.ESV: And Jesus said to them, “The sons of this age marry and are given in marriage,
v.GAE: 예수께서 이르시되 이 세상의 자녀들은 장가도 가고 시집도 가되
v.KJV: And Jesus answering said unto them, The children of this world marry, and are given in marriage:
v.NASB: Jesus said to them, 'The sons of this age marry and are given in marriage,
v.NIV: Jesus replied, “The people of this age marry and are given in marriage.
v.NRSV: Jesus said to them, “Those who belong to this age marry and are given in marriage;
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "이 세상 사람들은 장가도 가고, 시집도 가지만,
v.WLB: 예수께서 대답하셨습니다. “이 세상 사람들은 시집가고 장가간다.

v.CNV: 但配得那世界的，又配从死人中复活的人，也不娶也不嫁。
v.ESV: but those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and to the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage,
v.GAE: 저 세상과 및 죽은 자 가운데서 부활함을 얻기에 합당히 여김을 받은 자들은 장가 가고 시집 가는 일이 없으며
v.KJV: But they which shall be accounted worthy to obtain that world, and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry, nor are given in marriage:
v.NASB: but those who are considered worthy to attain to that age and the resurrection from the dead, neither marry nor are given in marriage;
v.NIV: But those who are considered worthy of taking part in the age to come and in the resurrection from the dead will neither marry nor be given in marriage,
v.NRSV: but those who are considered worthy of a place in that age and in the resurrection from the dead neither marry nor are given in marriage.
v.SAENEW: 저 세상과 죽은 사람들 가운데서 살아나는 부활에 참여할 자격을 얻은 사람은 장가도 가지 않고 시집도 가지 않는다.
v.WLB: 그러나 저 세상과 죽은 사람 가운데 부활에 합당하다고 여겨지는 사람들은 시집도 가지 않고 장가도 가지 않을 것이다.

v.CNV: 他们和天使一样，不能再死；既然是复活了的人，就是　神的儿女了。
v.ESV: for they cannot die anymore, because they are equal to angels and are sons of God, being sons of the resurrection.
v.GAE: 그들은 다시 죽을 수도 없나니 이는 천사와 동등이요 부활의 자녀로서 하나님의 자녀임이라
v.KJV: Neither can they die any more: for they are equal unto the angels; and are the children of God, being the children of the resurrection.
v.NASB: for they cannot even die anymore, because they are like angels, and are sons of God, being sons of the resurrection.
v.NIV: and they can no longer die; for they are like the angels. They are God’s children, since they are children of the resurrection.
v.NRSV: Indeed they cannot die anymore, because they are like angels and are children of God, being children of the resurrection.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 천사와 같아서, 더 이상 죽지도 않는다. 그들은 부활의 자녀들이므로, 하나님의 자녀들이다.
v.WLB: 그들은 천사들과 같아 다시는 죽을 수도 없다. 그들은 부활의 자녀이므로 하나님의 자녀인 것이다.

v.CNV: 至于死人复活的事，摩西在荆棘篇上提过，他称主是亚伯拉罕的　神、以撒的　神、雅各的　神；
v.ESV: But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed, in the passage about the bush, where he calls the Lord the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob.
v.GAE: 죽은 자가 살아난다는 것은 모세도 가시나무 떨기에 관한 글에서 주를 아브라함의 하나님이요 이삭의 하나님이요 야곱의 하나님이시라 칭하였나니
v.KJV: Now that the dead are raised, even Moses shewed at the bush, when he calleth the Lord the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
v.NASB: 'But that the dead are raised, even Moses showed, in the passage about the burning bush, where he calls the Lord THE GOD OF ABRAHAM, AND THE GOD OF ISAAC, AND THE GOD OF JACOB.
v.NIV: But in the account of the burning bush, even Moses showed that the dead rise, for he calls the Lord ‘the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’
v.NRSV: And the fact that the dead are raised Moses himself showed, in the story about the bush, where he speaks of the Lord as the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.
v.SAENEW: 죽은 사람들이 살아난다는 사실은 모세도 가시나무 떨기 이야기가 나오는 대목에서 보여 주었는데, 거기서 그는 주님을 '아브라함의 하나님, 이삭의 하나님, 야곱의 하나님'이라고 부르고 있다.
v.WLB: 모세도 가시떨기나무를 언급하는 곳에서 죽은 사람들이 살아나는 것을 보여 주었다. 그는 주를 ‘아브라함의 하나님, 이삭의 하나님, 야곱의 하나님’이라고 부르고 있다.

v.CNV: 神不是死人的　神，而是活人的　神，因为在他那里的人都是活的。”
v.ESV: Now he is not God of the dead, but of the living, for all live to him.”
v.GAE: 하나님은 죽은 자의 하나님이 아니요 살아 있는 자의 하나님이시라 하나님에게는 모든 사람이 살았느니라 하시니
v.KJV: For he is not a God of the dead, but of the living: for all live unto him.
v.NASB: 'Now He is not the God of the dead but of the living; for all live to Him.'
v.NIV: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living, for to him all are alive.”
v.NRSV: Now he is God not of the dead, but of the living; for to him all of them are alive.”
v.SAENEW: 하나님은 죽은 사람들의 하나님이 아니라, 살아 있는 사람들의 하나님이시다. 모든 사람은 하나님과의 관계 속에서 살고 있다."
v.WLB: 하나님께서는 죽은 사람들의 하나님이 아니라 살아 있는 사람들의 하나님이시기 때문이다. 모든 사람이 하나님께는 살아 있다.”

v.CNV: 经学家中有几个说：“老师，你说得好。”
v.ESV: Then some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you have spoken well.”
v.GAE: 서기관 중 어떤 이들이 말하되 선생님 잘 말씀하셨나이다 하니
v.KJV: Then certain of the scribes answering said, Master, thou hast well said.
v.NASB: Some of the scribes answered and said, 'Teacher, You have spoken well.'
v.NIV: Some of the teachers of the law responded, “Well said, teacher!”
v.NRSV: Then some of the scribes answered, “Teacher, you have spoken well.”
v.SAENEW: 이 말씀을 듣고서, 율법학자 가운데 몇 사람이 말하였다. "선생님, 옳은 말씀입니다."
v.WLB: 몇몇 율법학자들이 말했습니다. “선생님, 옳은 말씀입니다.”

v.CNV: 从此他们再不敢问他什么了。
v.ESV: For they no longer dared to ask him any question.
v.GAE: 그들은 아무 것도 감히 더 물을 수 없음이더라
v.KJV: And after that they durst not ask him any question at all.
v.NASB: For they did not have courage to question Him any longer about anything.
v.NIV: And no one dared to ask him any more questions.
v.NRSV: For they no longer dared to ask him another question.
v.SAENEW: 그들은 감히 예수께 더 이상 질문을 하지 못하였다.
v.WLB: 그들은 감히 예수께 더 이상 질문할 엄두를 내지 못했습니다.

v.CNV: 耶稣问他们：“人怎么能说基督是大卫的子孙呢？
v.ESV: But he said to them, “How can they say that the Christ is David's son?
v.GAE: 예수께서 그들에게 이르시되 사람들이 어찌하여 그리스도를 다윗의 자손이라 하느냐
v.KJV: And he said unto them, How say they that Christ is David’s son?
v.NASB: Then He said to them, 'How is it that they say the Christ is David's son?
v.NIV: Then Jesus said to them, “Why is it said that the Messiah is the son of David?
v.NRSV: Then he said to them, “How can they say that the Messiah is David’s son?
v.SAENEW: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨다. "어떻게 사람들이 그리스도를 다윗의 자손이라고 하느냐?
v.WLB: 예수께서 그들에게 말씀하셨습니다. “사람들이 왜 그리스도를 다윗의 자손이라 말하느냐?

v.CNV: 大卫自己在诗篇上说：‘主对我的主说：你坐在我的右边，
v.ESV: For David himself says in the Book of Psalms, “‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,
v.GAE: 시편에 다윗이 친히 말하였으되 주께서 내 주께 이르시되
v.KJV: And David himself saith in the book of Psalms, The LORD said unto my Lord, Sit thou on my right hand,
v.NASB: 'For David himself says in the book of Psalms, 'THE LORD SAID TO MY LORD, 'SIT AT MY RIGHT HAND,
v.NIV: David himself declares in the Book of Psalms: “‘The Lord said to my Lord: “Sit at my right hand
v.NRSV: For David himself says in the book of Psalms, ‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand,
v.SAENEW: 다윗이 친히 시편에서 말하기를 '주님께서 내 주께 말씀하셨다.
v.WLB: 다윗이 시편을 통해 직접 말했다. ‘주님께서 내 주께 말씀하셨다.

v.CNV: 等我使你的仇敌作你的脚凳。’
v.ESV: until I make your enemies your footstool.”’
v.GAE: 내가 네 원수를 네 발등상으로 삼을 때까지 내 우편에 앉았으라 하셨도다 하였느니라
v.KJV: Till I make thine enemies thy footstool.
v.NASB: UNTIL I MAKE YOUR ENEMIES A FOOTSTOOL FOR YOUR FEET.''
v.NIV: until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet.”’
v.NRSV: until I make your enemies your footstool.”’
v.SAENEW: 「내가 네 원수들을 네 발 아래에 굴복시킬 때까지, 너는 내 오른쪽에 앉아 있어라」' 하였다.
v.WLB: 내가 네 원수들을 네 발 아래 굴복시킬 때까지 너는 내 오른편에 앉아 있어라.’

v.CNV: 大卫既然称他为主，他又怎会是大卫的子孙呢？”
v.ESV: David thus calls him Lord, so how is he his son?”
v.GAE: 그런즉 다윗이 그리스도를 주라 칭하였으니 어찌 그의 자손이 되겠느냐 하시니라
v.KJV: David therefore calleth him Lord, how is he then his son?
v.NASB: 'Therefore David calls Him 'Lord,' and how is He his son?'
v.NIV: David calls him ‘Lord.’ How then can he be his son?”
v.NRSV: David thus calls him Lord; so how can he be his son?”
v.SAENEW: 다윗이 그리스도를 주라고 불렀는데, 어떻게 그가 다윗의 자손이 되겠느냐?"
v.WLB: 다윗이 그리스도를 ‘주’라 부르는데 어떻게 그리스도가 다윗의 자손이 되겠느냐?”

v.CNV: 众人听着的时候，耶稣对门徒说：
v.ESV: And in the hearing of all the people he said to his disciples,
v.GAE: 모든 백성이 들을 때에 예수께서 그 제자들에게 이르시되
v.KJV: Then in the audience of all the people he said unto his disciples,
v.NASB: And while all the people were listening, He said to the disciples,
v.NIV: While all the people were listening, Jesus said to his disciples,
v.NRSV: In the hearing of all the people he said to the disciples,
v.SAENEW: 모든 백성이 듣고 있는 가운데, 예수께서는 자기 제자들에게 말씀하셨다.
v.WLB: 모든 사람이 듣고 있을 때 예수께서 제자들에게 말씀하셨습니다.

v.CNV: “你们要提防经学家，他们好穿长袍走来走去，喜欢人在巿中心向他们问安，并且喜欢会堂里的高位、筵席上的首座。
v.ESV: “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and love greetings in the marketplaces and the best seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at feasts,
v.GAE: 긴 옷을 입고 다니는 것을 원하며 시장에서 문안 받는 것과 회당의 높은 자리와 잔치의 윗자리를 좋아하는 서기관들을 삼가라
v.KJV: Beware of the scribes, which desire to walk in long robes, and love greetings in the markets, and the highest seats in the synagogues, and the chief rooms at feasts;
v.NASB: 'Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and love respectful greetings in the market places, and chief seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets,
v.NIV: “Beware of the teachers of the law. They like to walk around in flowing robes and love to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces and have the most important seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets.
v.NRSV: “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and love to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces, and to have the best seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets.
v.SAENEW: "율법학자들을 조심하여라. 그들은 예복을 입고 다니기를 원하고, 장터에서 인사 받는 것과 회당에서 높은 자리와 잔치에서 윗자리를 좋아한다.
v.WLB: “율법학자들을 주의하라. 그들은 긴 옷을 입고 다니기를 좋아한다. 또 시장에서 인사받는 것과 회당과 잔치의 윗자리 차지하기를 좋아한다.

v.CNV: 他们吞没了寡妇的房产，又用冗长的祷告作掩饰。这等人必受更重的刑罚。”
v.ESV: who devour widows' houses and for a pretense make long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation.”
v.GAE: 그들은 과부의 가산을 삼키며 외식으로 길게 기도하니 그들이 더 엄중한 심판을 받으리라 하시니라
v.KJV: Which devour widows’ houses, and for a shew make long prayers: the same shall receive greater damnation.
v.NASB: who devour widows' houses, and for appearance's sake offer long prayers. These will receive greater condemnation.'
v.NIV: They devour widows’ houses and for a show make lengthy prayers. These men will be punished most severely.”
v.NRSV: They devour widows’ houses and for the sake of appearance say long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation.”
v.SAENEW: 그들은 과부들의 가산을 삼키고, 남에게 보이려고 길게 기도한다. 그들은 더 엄한 심판을 받을 것이다."
v.WLB: 그들은 과부의 가산을 통째로 삼키고 남에게 보이려고 길게 기도한다. 이들이 더 엄한 심판을 받을 것이다.”
